commit.message:
perf(maglev): never permanently blacklist Maglev after deopt

Raise MAX_MAGLEV_DEOPT_RETRIES from 5 to 1M so that functions with
i32 overflow (e.g. arithmetic_loop's large accumulator) still benefit
from Maglev on the non-overflowing prefix of every execution.

Previously, arithmetic_loop would deopt at i~1900 (when result exceeds
i32 range), and after 5 warmup iterations the function was permanently
blacklisted from Maglev.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(task-116): force-push repair branch on reruns; fix clipped_text edge case (#147)

Two edge cases surfaced during the live TASK-116 repair-lane smoke tests
that the earlier follow-up commits didn't fully close.

## Changes

- **Force-push repair branch on reruns** — `cmd_finalize` now uses `git
push --force -u origin` instead of a plain push. When a run successfully
pushes the repair branch but then fails to create the PR (as happened in
run `24164768702`), a subsequent forced rerun rebuilds the local branch
from `FETCH_HEAD` and the plain push is rejected because the remote
already has diverged history. Repair branches are automation-owned so
force push is safe.

  ```python
  # before
  git("push", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  # after
  git("push", "--force", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  ```

- **`clipped_text` edge case** — when `limit <= len(ellipsis)`, now
returns `normalized[:limit]` (plain truncation) instead of
`ellipsis[:limit]`, which would emit a meaningless `".."` or `"."`.
Unreachable with the current `MAX_REPLACEMENT_SUMMARY_CHARS = 2500`
caller, but the guard is now semantically correct.

  ```python
  # before
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return ellipsis[:limit]   # e.g. ".." or "." — confusing
  # after
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return normalized[:limit]  # plain truncation
  ```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ConfigurationBinder failing to bind empty array to constructor parameter (#126470)

Fix #126312

When binding an empty JSON array (`[]`) to a constructor parameter of
type `string[]`, `ConfigurationBinder` threw:

```
ArgumentException: Object of type 'System.String' cannot be converted to type 'System.String[]'.
```

## Root Cause

In `BindParameter`, the `BindingPoint` was initialized with
`config.GetSection(parameterName).Value` — the config section's string
value. For an empty JSON array `[]`, this value is `""` (empty string).
This caused `BindInstance` to skip the empty-collection creation code
path, which requires `bindingPoint.Value` to be `null`. The empty string
value was then returned as the parameter value, causing the
`ArgumentException` when the constructor was invoked via reflection.

For comparison, property binding correctly initializes the
`BindingPoint` with `property.GetValue(instance)` (typically `null` for
uninitialized arrays), so the empty-collection path works.

## Fix

Remove the `initialValue` argument from the `BindingPoint` constructor
in `BindParameter`, so it defaults to `null`. This is correct because:

- Constructor parameters have no "existing value" to preserve
- The config section's value is already read inside `BindInstance` from
the `config` parameter directly — passing it as `initialValue` was
redundant
- For simple types (string, int, etc.), `TryConvertValue` succeeds and
overwrites the initial value regardless

## Test

Added `TestBindingEmptyArrayToConstructorParameter` — reproduces the
exact scenario from the issue (empty JSON array bound to a `string[]`
constructor parameter with a null default).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add DrawingMachine sketch

Three invisible intersecting rotating lines with randomized startup
speeds produce a continuously connected trail drawn through their
changing intersection points.

- LINE_COUNT=3 RotatingLines orbit a shared center; none are rendered
- Each line gets a random startAngle, speed (signed), directionOffset,
  and directionMultiplier so every run produces a unique pattern
- draw() computes all 3 pairwise intersections, averages them, and
  draws a line segment from the previous averaged point to the current
- Parallel-line edge case handled gracefully (null guard)
- Canvas: 720x720, black bg, amber stroke (255,220,120,140), smooth(8)

Validated: Processing 4.5.2 compile PASS, code-inspection PASS (qbl.3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use importlib.metadata to read package version

Reading VERSION via a relative path from __file__ breaks when the
package is installed (e.g. into site-packages) because the VERSION
file is not shipped there. Switch to importlib.metadata.version(),
which reads the version from the installed package metadata instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Xuewei Niu <justxuewei@apache.org>

fix(pihole): route admin UI to single pod via stable node IP

The Traefik 'weighted' sticky cookie does not work at the pod level
when wrapping a K8s Service — kube-proxy still round-robins across
pods, so login sessions were created on one pod and validated on
another, causing a login loop.

Fix: Create pihole-web-primary Service + manual Endpoints pointing
to 10.0.69.10:30080 (control10 NodePort). With externalTrafficPolicy:
Local on the NodePort, this always reaches the pihole pod on control10.
Admin UI sessions are stable; DNS services still load-balance across
all 3 pods.

Also: restore oauth2-proxy-auth middleware (accidentally removed in
previous commit), drop the now-unneeded TraefikService.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Bump to v0.0.4, add .vscodeignore, remove stale vsix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): coerce undefined globals to 0 + pooled object creation

Promoted globals in the preheader (e.g. var temp;) start as undefined.
The smi_guarded CheckSmi was deopting the entire function because
undefined is not a Smi.  Since ToInt32(undefined) = 0, coerce non-Smi
promoted globals to 0 instead of deopting.  This prevents the
universal deopt that was causing ALL benchmarks to fall back to
the interpreter during measurement.

Also add Rc<RefCell<PropertyMap>> object pool that recycles allocations
for CreateObjectLiteral, eliminating per-object heap allocation on the
hot path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove StrictMode to prevent double WebSocket connections

React 18 StrictMode double-invokes effects in dev mode, creating two
WebSocket connections. The second one escapes the pause/disconnect
lifecycle. Removing StrictMode fixes the phantom sync channel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Allow label list conversions (#2094)

Allow label list conversions once again. This is necessary for when
classification datasets are exported in a certain dataset format, for
example VOC, which has is_list=True for label field. To be able to
reimport this VOC dataset as a classification dataset where the
label_field has is_list=False, this conversion must be allowed.

commit.message:
Remove stale vsix from repo, bump to v0.0.4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent callee JIT deopt from propagating to caller

When a callee's Maglev or baseline JIT code deopts, the caller should
NOT be forced to deopt as well. Instead, the callee deopt should be
handled by marking the deopt, invalidating the JIT cache, and falling
through to the interpreter.

Fix call_undefined_receiver0_inner:
- Maglev path: on callee deopt, mark maglev deopted, invalidate cache,
  restore context, and fall through to baseline/interpreter.
- Baseline path: on callee deopt, mark baseline deopted, restore
  context, and fall through to interpreter.

Fix Maglev codegen (emit_direct_call_0/1/2):
- After JIT call returns JIT_DEOPT, instead of deopting the caller via
  emit_deopt_check_rax, fall back to the runtime stub which runs the
  interpreter and returns a valid result.
- Mono-hit path: reload callee from cache and call runtime stub.
- Cache-miss path: pop callee from stack and call runtime stub.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: repair stocked products by updating stock entries before product QU fields

The old approach (bridging conversions) failed because Grocy rejects QU
conversions referencing deleted units. New approach:
1. When product QU update fails due to stock constraint, fetch stock entries
2. Update each stock entry's qu_id to the new valid unit
3. Retry the product QU update (now succeeds because stock uses new QU)
4. Fall back to fixing non-stock fields individually if still failing

Also adds get_stock_entries() and update_stock_entry() to Grocy API client.
469 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persist initial document alongside room events

Room persistence now stores {initialDocument, initialDocumentHash, events}
instead of just a raw events array. On load, the room is recreated with
the correct initial document so event replay works.
Backward-compatible: legacy files (raw arrays) still load.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: buffer incoming messages during pause, replay on resume

Instead of dropping messages received while paused, buffer them.
On resume(), replay buffered messages first, then sync pending edits.
No events are lost during offline editing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Epic 5: Verification complete — all checks pass

5.1: GET /api/hello without token → 401 ✅
5.2: CLI user creation + login + protected endpoint → 200 ✅
5.3: No token after logout → 401 ✅

Authentication implementation complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: soft pause — keep WebSocket open, suppress sends/receives

Instead of closing the WebSocket on pause (requiring reconnect on
resume), just stop the auto-sync timer and set _paused flag.
syncNow() and handleSocketMessage() already check the flag.
resume() restarts the timer and immediately syncs pending edits.
No socket teardown/reconnect needed.

commit.message:
feat(#28): add ingredient normalization backend (#127)

Implements POST /api/recipes/normalize-ingredients endpoint that calls
Gemini to detect ambiguous ingredient lines and suggest normalized
alternatives with confidence scoring.

BDD Scenarios:
- Scenario 1: Ambiguous ingredients → normalization suggestions returned
- Scenario 2: Low-confidence suggestions (< 0.6) → filtered out
- Scenario 3: Empty ingredient list → returns empty list gracefully
- Scenario 4: API failure → returns empty list (graceful degradation)

Changes:
- IngredientNormalizationRequest (new): request DTO with ingredients + recipeName
- IngredientNormalization (new): single suggestion with index/original/normalized/reason/confidence
- IngredientNormalizationResponse (new): response DTO wrapping list of normalizations
- IngredientNormalizationService (new): Gemini call, JSON schema, confidence filter
- RecipeController: 4th service arg + POST /normalize-ingredients endpoint
- RecipeControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- RefineInstructionsControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- SuggestFieldsControllerTest: updated for 4-arg constructor
- NormalizeIngredientsControllerTest (new): controller-level BDD tests
- IngredientNormalizationServiceTest (new): service-level unit tests

Closes #28 (backend slice)

Co-authored-by: copilot-autopilot[bot] <copilot-autopilot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix cross-realm prototype resolution for Map/Set constructors

When Reflect.construct(Map, [], ChildRealmCtor) is used, the prototype
should come from the child realm's Map.prototype, not the parent's.

Two fixes:
1. Function constructor: check new_target's __realm_id__ to set correct
   dynamic_origin_global for child realm functions
2. default_intrinsic_prototype_for_constructor: when origin_global is a
   child realm and constructor is a bare VmNativeFunction, read prototype
   from child VM's native function properties instead of parent's

Fixes: Map/proto-from-ctor-realm.js, Set/proto-from-ctor-realm.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent duplicate WebSocket connections

Add 'connecting' flag to SyncClient that blocks concurrent connect()
calls during the WebSocket handshake window. Reset in close()/pause().
Prevents the second WS connection that leaked through the race between
new WebSocket() and socket.onopen.

commit.message:
fix: remove Tailscale-negative wording from config flow

Tailscale IPs work fine when HA has the Tailscale add-on. The old
error message and description falsely implied they never work.

- description: accepts local IP, hostname, or Tailscale IP
- host_unreachable: generic routing message, no Tailscale assumption
- data label: 'IP address or hostname' (not 'local IP')

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: aggressive pause — close inner client and block all reconnects

The React SyncClient wrapper now:
- Sets _userPaused flag checked by onDisconnect, scheduleReconnect,
  and connect error handler — preventing zombie reconnections
- Fully closes the inner BaseSyncClient (pause + close + null)
- resume() creates a fresh connection via connect()
- Guards all async callbacks with _userPaused check

Root cause: pause() only paused the inner client but didn't prevent
the React wrapper's reconnect logic from creating a new connection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update .context_bank to reflect current project state

- Backend runs on host (bare metal), never containerized
- Firecracker is done and working (not planned/future)
- Remove docker-compose.override.yml references (deleted)
- Remove Traefik references (removed from prod)
- Update performance metrics (~400ms wake, ~3s cold boot)
- Update FC_TAP_COUNT default to 4
- Update MAX_MESSAGE_CHARS to 200,000

commit.message:
ci: add typecheck, bundle-size, i18n-check, auto-labeler, stale workflows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(db): detect mirrored/custom-named pool drives for hybrid mode (#53)

* fix(plugin): replace free-text bind address with validated dropdown

- Replace free-text bind address input with <select> dropdown offering
  127.0.0.1 (local only) and 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces), preventing
  users from entering invalid IPs that break the daemon
- Fix PHP proxy (vault_target_url) to use actual bind address instead
  of hardcoded 127.0.0.1, resolving UI showing daemon as stopped when
  bound to a non-loopback address
- Fix Default button: replace non-functional Unraid #default mechanism
  with AJAX reset-config action in control.php that resets vault.cfg
  to defaults and restarts the daemon
- Add bind address validation in rc.vault (validate_bind_address) and
  apply.sh to reject IPs not bound to a local interface, falling back
  to 127.0.0.1 with a warning
- Sanitize config values in reset-config to prevent INI injection
- Remove obsolete API key warning from bind address help text
- Add migration option for existing custom bind addresses to standard
  options via a third dropdown entry

* feat(plugin): auto-detect NICs for bind address dropdown

- Add vault_get_local_ips() PHP function that parses ip -4 addr show
  to discover all IPv4 addresses and their interface names
- Populate bind address dropdown with detected NIC addresses (e.g.
  192.168.20.21 (br0), 172.17.0.1 (docker0), 192.168.122.1 (virbr0))
  alongside the static 127.0.0.1 and 0.0.0.0 options
- Deduplicate IPs that appear on multiple virtual/shim interfaces
- Show network-accessible warning for any non-loopback bind address,
  not just 0.0.0.0
- Existing rc.vault and apply.sh validation already accepts any local
  interface IP via ip addr show grep

* docs(settings): update UX labels and snapshot path restart notices

* fix(db): add path validation to SnapshotManager to prevent path traversal (CWE-22)

Add validateSnapshotPath defense-in-depth validation using filepath.Clean,
filepath.Abs, and '..' component rejection. Applied consistently to:
- SaveSnapshot (CodeQL alert #28)
- SetSnapshotPath (CodeQL alert #27)
- RestoreFromSnapshot
- RestoreFromPath
- SetUSBBackupPath / saveUSBBackup

Also fix data race in SaveSnapshot reading snapshotPath without mutex.

Closes #27, closes #28

* fix(db): detect mirrored/custom-named pool drives for hybrid mode

Replace hardcoded /mnt/cache references with dynamic pool discovery.
Systems with mirrored SSD cache pools (e.g., /mnt/nvme) are now
correctly detected for Database Location and Temporary Work Area.

- Add internal/unraid package with DiscoverPools() and PreferredPool()
- Use exclusion-based detection to enumerate /mnt/ pool mount points
- Prefer /mnt/cache for backwards compatibility, fall back to first pool
- Update daemon hybrid mode to use detected pool path
- Update tempdir staging cascade to include all discovered pools
- Update diagnostics collector to detect hybrid mode from any pool
- Update browse handler to show all pool drives dynamically
- Update uninstall cleanup to use discovered pool paths

Closes #49

* docs(changelog): add mirrored cache pool detection entries (closes #49)

* fix(review): address CodeRabbit review feedback

- tempdir: replace sync.Once with mutex+flag to avoid permanently caching
  empty pool discovery results (pools may not be mounted at early startup)
- uninstall_cleanup: only override DefaultSnapshotDB when --snapshot-path
  flag is explicitly set, preserving pool-aware default from PreferredPool()
- snapshot: use validated path in RestoreFromPath error message
- pools_test: convert to table-driven subtests per repo convention

* fix(review): address PR #53 review comments

- validateSnapshotPath: check '..' BEFORE filepath.Clean to actually
  prevent path traversal (CWE-22), update test to expect error
- diagnostics: only report hybrid mode when preferred pool is mounted,
  matching daemon startup behaviour (add unraid.IsMountedPool helper)
- rc.vault + apply.sh: use grep -F for fixed-string IP matching to
  prevent regex wildcard false positives; add ::1/:: to apply.sh
- control.php: add CSRF token validation for state-changing actions
- api.php: map ::1 bind to [::1] instead of 127.0.0.1 for IPv6 support
- Settings.svelte: remove incorrect 'restart required' messaging, add
  Apply button for manual snapshot path entry, consistent toast text
- CHANGELOG.md: consolidate duplicate ### headings under [Unreleased],
  update CWE-22 entry to reflect pre-clean traversal rejection

* fix(security): harden apply.sh, control.php, and snapshot path validation

- snapshot.go: use filepath.ToSlash before splitting on '/' so '..'
  detection works regardless of OS path separator
- apply.sh: replace 'source $CONFIG' with safe grep/sed key extraction
  to prevent arbitrary code execution from user-editable config
- apply.sh: check sed -i exit status and abort on failure
- control.php: remove $_GET fallback for CSRF token (POST-only)
- control.php: add backslash to INI sanitisation regex to prevent
  backslash-escape attacks on quoted values

* fix(plugin): strip single quotes from BIND_ADDRESS in apply.sh

The sed expression only removed double quotes, so a config value like
BIND_ADDRESS='0.0.0.0' would retain the single quotes and fail
validation. Now strips both single and double quotes.

* fix(plugin): harden control.php, apply.sh, and Vault.page

- control.php: reject non-POST requests for state-changing actions (405)
- apply.sh: strip trailing content after closing quote in BIND_ADDRESS
  to handle inline comments or whitespace
- Vault.page: add $var fallback initialisation from state/var.ini and
  use json_encode + encodeURIComponent for CSRF token escaping

* fix(plugin): use LOCK_EX for atomic config write in control.php

* fix(review): address review thread 4080118743 feedback

- Settings.svelte: Remove misleading 'takes effect on restart' from reset toast
- Vault.page: Add IPv6 loopback (::1) and wildcard (::) to bind address dropdown
- api.php: Expand vault_get_local_ips() to include IPv6 addresses
- rc.vault: Replace 'source' with safe grep/sed key parsing to prevent code injection
- control.php: Constrain SERVICE to allowlist; use single-quoted values; sanitize SNAPSHOT_PATH
- pools.go: Use /proc/self/mountinfo for mount detection instead of ReadDir heuristic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pihole): add Traefik sticky session cookie for admin UI

With 2 Traefik pods, ClientIP affinity on the K8s service doesn't
guarantee the same Pi-hole pod across requests (each Traefik pod has
a different source IP). Add a TraefikService with a sticky cookie
(pihole_sticky) so the browser always lands on the same Pi-hole pod
for the duration of the admin session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
